success_surface: Cell-level bin location counting with custom formatting requirements
effective_behaviors: Perform exploratory inspection of spreadsheet structure before implementing solution logic
Encapsulate complex string parsing/filtering logic in helper functions with edge case handling
Apply all requested formatting styles (font, size, borders) in combination with data writes
Save output to new file path without modifying source file
Log processed results to verify correctness after execution
Handle multi-value cell content with delimiter-based splitting and whitespace trimming
evidence: Agent executed inspection code first (sheet names, columns, rows, sample row data) before solution code
Created count_valid_bins() function that checks for None, splits by comma, strips whitespace, and validates first character
Applied Font(name='Courier New', size=9) and Border with all four sides set to 'thin' style
Saved to '/mnt/data/outputs/...' path rather than overwriting source at '/mnt/data/spreadsheet/...'
Printed final results showing C2:C6 values after processing completed
Used str(location_string).split(',') approach for multi-location cell fields with comma separation

memory_items.title: Pre-Implementation Structure Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always examine spreadsheet structure (sheet names, column headers, row counts, sample data) before coding the solution.
memory_items.generalization: For any spreadsheet task, begin with diagnostic queries using wb.sheetnames, ws.max_row, ws.max_column, and sample cell reads to confirm assumptions about data layout.

memory_items.title: Complex Cell Content Parsing
memory_items.lesson: When cells contain delimited multi-values, parse safely with delimiter split, whitespace trim, and empty-value filtering.
memory_items.generalization: Use str.split(delimiter), strip() on each element, filter out empty strings, and handle None/NaN values before applying filtering logic to individual items.

memory_items.title: Combined Formatting Application
memory_items.lesson: Apply all specified visual formatting properties together with data writes in the same operation loop.
memory_items.generalization: When instruction includes font, size, border, color, or alignment requirements, apply them immediately after writing values using the appropriate style objects from openpyxl.styles.

memory_items.title: Verification Through Result Logging
memory_items.lesson: After writing to target cells, log the actual values to confirm correctness before finishing.
memory_items.generalization: Include post-processing verification loops that read back modified cells and print their contents to provide visible confirmation of successful manipulation.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Structure Inspection First**: Before coding any manipulation, query sheet names, dimensions, headers, and sample row data to validate assumptions about layout.
- **Delimited Content Safety**: When parsing multi-value cell content, use delimiter-split with strip() on each token, filter empty strings, and guard against None/NaN input.
- **Format-Before-Save Pattern**: Apply all required styling (fonts, borders, colors) in the same iteration where data is written to maintain consistency.
- **Post-Write Verification**: Read back modified cells after processing and log their values to confirm the manipulation succeeded before declaring completion.
